creator: cmt@freebsd.org
text:
If nobody else will step up...
Is this still required? (I can't directly see anything on their website about this).
Funny enough, recent PostgreSQL versions do not support floating-point timestamps anymore: https://git.postgresql.org/gitweb/?p=postgresql.git;a=commitdiff;h=b6aa17e0ae367afdcea07118e016111af4fa6bc3
In short: in PostgreSQL 10, we only have integer timetamps, and the floating-point timestamps have problems.
I vote for rejection in this case.
